privileged_info_summary: Confirmed that the task correctly utilizes the real San Francisco UrbanSim dataset (sanfran_public.h5) from UDST, and explicitly verified the expected output paths via task scripts. I downloaded the real HDF5 data and ran the exact data-processing logic provided in export_result.sh to establish the ground truth internally: the lowest income decile (gt_d1) has a median space consumption of 780.34 sqft/person, while the highest income decile (gt_d10) has 476.00 sqft/person, resulting in an expected top-to-bottom space ratio of 0.6100. Furthermore, there are 183 qualifying zones (>= 50 households). Additionally, discovered that the task is defective because 'verifier.py' is entirely missing from the environment, making the task technically unscorable despite the export script executing successfully.
